test_cases.description: European Spanish VO narration — fishing industry collapse, mixed timing constraints
test_cases.input_summary: 3-line narration excerpt, off-screen VO, ES target, mixed timing (3–6 sec blocks), one over-long translation
test_cases.output_summary: Two-column adapted script with syllable counts, one flagged line (NR_02 addition removed), prose summary

test_cases.description: Brazilian Portuguese — nature documentary, tight 2-second blocks with on-screen narrator
test_cases.input_summary: 4-line script, narrator visible on screen, PT-BR target, very short blocks (2.0–2.5 sec), source translation systematically 20% over
test_cases.output_summary: Aggressive contraction of all 4 lines, 3 flagged for semantic trimming, note about plosive avoidance at visible mouth-close moments

test_cases.description: German — historical documentary, compound-noun-heavy, 5–8 second blocks
test_cases.input_summary: 5-line script, DE target, long blocks, German compound terminology that cannot be contracted; one block requires content decision by producer
test_cases.output_summary: 3 lines adapted cleanly, 2 flagged with [TIMING NOTE] citing compound-noun constraint, summary recommends producer decision on flagged lines before recording

notes: Strong performance across all dimensions. Professionalism and relevance highest — the two-column format with syllable counts is immediately usable by a dubbing editor. Fluency score reflects that adapted target-language lines cannot be fully verified by an English-only evaluator; real-world use requires native-speaker review.
